seo.title: Llama Stack Guardrails for Legal
seo.description: Compliance guide for Llama Stack agents in legal. Meet Attorney-client privilege protections, Bar association ethics rules requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Legal teams adopting Llama Stack for document review, case research, and client advisory face heightened risks due to the framework's agentic loops, memory retrieval, and tool integrations that can hallucinate citations or mishandle privileged data. Compliance guardrails are essential to prevent malpractice from false positives in safety filtering, schema-mismatched tool responses, and fabricated case associations. Vex provides post-processing verification to ensure Llama Stack outputs meet bar association ethics and court filing standards.

complianceRequirements.description: Client confidential information must never be exposed, stored in model memory banks, or returned in agent responses.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Scans Llama Stack memory retrievals and agent outputs for PII, privileged keywords, and client identifiers before delivery.

complianceRequirements.regulation: Bar association ethics rules (ABA Model Rule 1.1)
complianceRequirements.description: AI-generated legal content must be competent, verified, and human-reviewed by licensed attorneys.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Flags hallucinated citations, unverified case law, and incomplete legal reasoning patterns in agent responses.

complianceRequirements.description: All filings must contain verifiable citations to real cases; fabricated citations lead to sanctions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Validates case citations against legal databases and flags Mata v. Avianca-style hallucinations.

complianceRequirements.description: AI document classification must be auditable and free from schema errors in tool responses.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Verifies tool response schemas conform to expected eDiscovery formats and detects privilege misclassifications.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated case citations
industryRisks.description: Llama Stack memory retrieval fabricates non-existent case law references.
industryRisks.example: Agent cites 'Mata v. Avianca, 2023' which doesn't exist, leading to court sanctions.

industryRisks.name: Privilege data leakage
industryRisks.description: Safety shield false positives block legitimate privileged document review while missing real leaks.
industryRisks.example: Client SSN appears in agent research summary sent to opposing counsel.

industryRisks.name: Tool schema violations
industryRisks.description: eDiscovery tools return malformed JSON breaking court filing pipelines.
industryRisks.example: Document classifier returns string instead of required relevance score object.

industryRisks.name: Agent loop exhaustion
industryRisks.description: Turn limits exceeded without resolution, delaying critical filings.
industryRisks.example: Case brief research loops 50+ turns without producing draft.

implementation.title: Verify Citation Integrity Post-Agent Turn
implementation.description: Wrap Llama Stack agent responses with vex.verify() to catch hallucinated case citations before filing.
implementation.code:
import vex

from llama_stack_client import LlamaStackAgent

agent = LlamaStackAgent()

@ vex.verify(policies=[
    'legal.citation.verifiable',
    'legal.hallucination.block'
])
def verify_legal_response(response):
    return agent.process_turn(response)

# Usage
research_query = "Find precedents for contract breach in California"
verified_brief = verify_legal_response(research_query)

print(verified_brief['citations'])  # Only verified cases pass

implementation.title: Validate eDiscovery Tool Schemas
implementation.description: Ensure Llama Stack tool responses conform to court-mandated eDiscovery JSON schemas.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Llama Stack in Legal: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
A mid-sized California firm deployed Llama Stack for M&A due diligence acceleration. The agent was configured with memory banks containing 10,000+ historical deal documents, Westlaw tool integration for case research, and safety shields for privilege detection. Paralegals fed contract batches into the system expecting relevance-ranked outputs with supporting precedents for negotiation leverage.

On the Smith Industries $250M merger, the agent confidently returned a 15-page memo citing 'Smith v. TechCorp, 2022 Cal. App.' as controlling precedent for non-compete clauses. The memo recommended aggressive negotiation positions based on this 'landmark' case. Filing deadlines loomed as partners reviewed the output for court submissions.
caseStudyScenario.outcome:
Vex guardrails caught the hallucinated citation during post-processing verification, blocking submission. The 'Smith v. TechCorp' case didn't exist—memory retrieval had fabricated the association from keyword patterns. Vex also flagged a privilege false negative where client financials leaked into the memo.

Manual verification found the correct precedent (actual 'TechCorp v. Smith, 2019'), enabling compliant filing. The firm avoided sanctions, implemented Vex on all Llama Stack workflows, and reduced review time 40% while maintaining bar compliance.

cta.heading: Comply with Legal Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex verification into your Llama Stack deployment today to eliminate hallucination risks and ensure court-ready outputs.
